programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: A rebellion in the north, fanned by the kings cousin Reginald Pole with intent to force England back to Rome, is spreading south. The rebels are demanding a stop to the religious reforms of the past three years, a return to Rome and Cromwells head, perceiving him to be the architect of their misfortunes. The royal adviser has been left undone by his experiences at Shaftesbury Abbey, and struggles to navigate the increasingly dangerous atmosphere at court

programme.title: Mrs Robinson
programme.desc: Former president of Ireland Mary Robinson tells her story, illuminating battles for justice and equality of over half a century - on the streets, in the courts, at the ballot box, and in backroom corridors of power. This documentary follows her journey from reforming constitutional lawyer and Senator to becoming a crusading UN High Commissioner

programme.title: Avoidance
programme.desc: On a trip to Yorkshire, Claire forces Jonathan to lie to her parents about their break-up, a lie that goes way too far. Dan and Courtney go on respective mums and dads nights out. The professor calls Dan asking for an answer on the job, so she seeks advice from her fellow dads. The nights are both very different, but Courtneys ends with a shock revelation as Dan makes a decision about her dream job. Comedy, guest starring Paula Wilcox, with Romesh Ranganathan, Jessica Knappett and Aisling Bea

programme.title: The Complaints Bureau
programme.desc: Consumer programme The Complaints Bureau returns for a new series on RTÉ One. Finance expert Eoin McGee sets the caseload for three consumer journalists advocating and arguing on behalf of members of the public who have hit brick walls. The trio - Conor Pope, Amy Molloy and Siobhan Maguire tackle problems big, small and seemingly intractable. Along the way there are frustrations, dead ends, awkward interviews, cautionary tales and, sometimes at least, successful results.

programme.desc: Hercule Poirot is on the trail of a serial killer who seemingly selects his victims and the locations of their deaths alphabetically, sending the Belgian sleuth a series of taunting letters announcing his next move and leaving a railway guidebook at the crime scenes as a calling card. programme.title: Reeling in the Years
programme.desc: Another dip into the archives for a retrospective of news and music from 1973. The general election provides the first change of government for 16 years, and a report published on the status of women in Ireland recommends radical changes to promote equality

programme.title: Nationwide
programme.desc: A look at the historic Deerfield House in Dublins Phoenix Park, residence of the US Ambassador to Ireland. Presented by Blathnaid Ni Chofaigh

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jasmine faces a crucial decision, Penny confronts Nicola, and Ross and Vicki discuss their future

programme.title: Eva Paus Asian Kitchen
programme.desc: Eva Pau begins by cooking Hong Kong style French toast, followed by a Vietnamese tiramisu cake, before finishing with aromatic duck pancakes

programme.title: Nevens Ulster Food Tour
programme.desc: Neven Maguire heads to Bundoran in Donegal to meet Irelands first professional surfer Richie Fitzgerald. They talk about the towns love of surfing and the strong influence it has had on Bundorans cafe culture. He also visits a family-run bakery on the edge of Laghey village, learns about hand-crafted gin in the Boatyard Distillery in Enniskillen and visits Belleek Pottery, where he has a go at creating and painting his own piece

programme.title: RTÉ News: Nine OClock and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: Blue Lights
programme.desc: Grace and Shane push boundaries for Lindsays sake as a threat closes in. Aisling and Tommy respond to a call-out where things are not as they seem, with unexpected consequences

programme.title: The Great House Revival
programme.desc: Jill Leavy trades her Dublin home hunt for a bold renovation, transforming her dads abandoned 1932 primary school in Coralstown into her first home

programme.title: Red Eye
programme.desc: Eighteen months after Russia is blamed for shooting down a new cargo plane over the Atlantic, DS Hana Li is summoned to investigate the murder of an American diplomatic courier at Heathrow. The investigation leads her to Londons US Embassy where new American Ambassador Ronald Tillman is hosting a party. Realising the killer has infiltrated the building, Hana and US Embassy security chief Clay Brody hunt them down. Meanwhile, a Russian threat is received onboard a UK Government jet. Thriller, starring Jing Lusi, Martin Compston and Lesley Sharp

programme.title: Any Given Day: Cork University Hospital
programme.desc: Following staff shifts and patient journeys across the hospital. Together, they tell a bigger story of Cork University Hospital on any given day

programme.title: On the Beat
programme.desc: Gardai in Limerick City carry out an extensive operation to put a stop to a gang of youths committing crimes on a regular basis, while Gardai in Monaghan deal with a busy Saturday night in the town

programme.title: The Last Irish Missionaries
programme.desc: Part one of two. At its peak in the 1960s, over 6,000 Irish Catholic missionaries were dispersed over 80 countries. In the modern day there are fewer than 500, marking the end of an era. Bryan Dobson and Dearbhail McDonald trace the beginning of the modern Irish missionary movement, meeting those who have returned to a changed Ireland after a life of service overseas and those who are still on the frontline in some of the worlds toughest environments

programme.title: High Road, Low Road
programme.desc: National treasures Daniel and Majella ODonnell head to the lively city of Zadar on Croatias Dalmatian coast. From a natural mud bath to a luxury spa, and a speed boat to a bell tower, one of them enjoys a luxury holiday, while the other explores the city on a budget

programme.title: Nevens Ulster Food Tour
programme.desc: Neven Maguire heads to Bundoran in Donegal to meet Irelands first professional surfer Richie Fitzgerald. They talk about the towns love of surfing and the strong influence it has had on Bundorans cafe culture. He also visits a family-run bakery on the edge of Laghey village, learns about hand-crafted gin in the Boatyard Distillery in Enniskillen and visits Belleek Pottery, where he has a go at creating and painting his own piece

programme.desc: Grace and Shane push boundaries for Lindsays sake as a threat closes in. Aisling and Tommy respond to a call-out where things are not as they seem, with unexpected consequences

programme.title: The Great House Revival
programme.desc: Jill Leavy trades her Dublin home hunt for a bold renovation, transforming her dads abandoned 1932 primary school in Coralstown into her first home

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Miss Higgins receives an unexpected visitor, and Sister Monica ruffles feathers. Poplar votes for its Mother of the Year

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: On Glenn and Petras wedding day, an ex-soldier with a bomb demands that Harry and Fergus solve the murder of his daughter, on the anniversary of her death

programme.title: On the Beat
programme.desc: Gardai in Limerick City carry out an extensive operation to put a stop to a gang of youths committing crimes on a regular basis, while Gardai in Monaghan deal with a busy Saturday night in the town

programme.title: The Last Irish Missionaries
programme.desc: Part one of two. At its peak in the 1960s, over 6,000 Irish Catholic missionaries were dispersed over 80 countries. In the modern day there are fewer than 500, marking the end of an era. Bryan Dobson and Dearbhail McDonald trace the beginning of the modern Irish missionary movement, meeting those who have returned to a changed Ireland after a life of service overseas and those who are still on the frontline in some of the worlds toughest environments

programme.title: High Road, Low Road
programme.desc: National treasures Daniel and Majella ODonnell head to the lively city of Zadar on Croatias Dalmatian coast. From a natural mud bath to a luxury spa, and a speed boat to a bell tower, one of them enjoys a luxury holiday, while the other explores the city on a budget

programme.title: Cheap Irish Homes
programme.desc: Maggie Molloy is on a house hunt with traditional wood worker Eoin Reardon, whos looking to buy an old single storey stone cottage that he can restore to its original state. Theyre joined by stone mason Dominic Keogh as Maggie guides them around County Mayo in search of the perfect property

programme.title: Home Rescue: The Big Fix
programme.desc: In Dublin, the team helps Ann OMalley sort out her cluttered three-bed semi in Clondalkin

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: With time running out, AC-12 attempt to unmask H, the fourth individual commanding the network of corrupt officers behind the murder of Gail Vella. However, sinister and powerful forces appear intent on orchestrating a cover-up. Steve Oram (Sightseers) guest stars in the final episode of the current series

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma has been dreaming of meeting her long-lost father ever since she was a little girl, it played out in her imagination like a black and white movie. After the death of her great-granny and Joans ongoing situation, Alma feels it might be time to make that dream a reality. She tracks down her father and spends time with him? but can reality really live up to the movies in her mind?

programme.title: Spent
programme.desc: Jos relationship is on the rocks, and when Mia arrives to pick up the pieces, their romantic tension comes to a head. Back at Chrissys, David is making himself at home just as Mias debts in America are catching up with her. A timely invite from Liv offers the perfect distraction, and at a swanky party, ex-lover Rusty has a much bigger escape on offer for her

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers tackle illusion macarons and a fruity favourite, before unleashing their creativity by making a dramatic 3D mask entirely from biscuits.

programme.title: For Peats Sake
programme.desc: The vital role turf has played in Ireland and the battle that ensued when the EU tried to stop Irish people cutting turf. For thousands of years people in Ireland have cut turf to heat their homes and cook their food. It was an accessible, affordable and vital fuel, made from the very soil of Ireland itself. Then in the 1990s people realised that there was a lot more to bogs than cheap fuel

programme.title: RFDS: Royal Flying Doctor Service

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr consider the kitchen. With a love for eating and entertaining, its crucial to the project that they get this social space perfect. They plan a Spanish-tiled extravaganza, but are initially unable to agree on a colour scheme. Alans sloppy bricklaying skills are put to the test when Scott ropes him in to try to build a traditional cooker hood, but Amanda is unimpressed with the result. The best friends head to Seville for a tapas tour at the citys gastronomic hub. Though not a fan of heights, Alan prepares to face his fears when invited on a rock-climbing course

programme.title: Hell for Leather: The Story of Gaelic Football
programme.desc: Cora Staunton, Jim McGuinness and Jack McCaffrey reveal the deep, personal sparks that ignited their passion for the game. Doing it for the community - and with the support of numerous mentors - it was the parish that first roared them all on. For many in the south, Gaelic Football was just a game but in Northern Ireland it went further, deeply enmeshed in identity and tribalism. This episode travels across the border to hear how the game and its players fared through a century of strife

programme.title: Live UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Paris Saint-Germain v Liverpool (Kick-off 8.00pm). Peter Collins presents all the action from the quarter-final first-leg tie at Parc des Princes. These teams met in the last 16 stage last term and the Reds claimed a 1-0 win here thanks to a goal by substitute Harvey Elliott, only for PSG to prevail by the same scoreline at Anfield and prevail on penalties. The French outfit went on to lift the trophy, and their 8-2 aggregate dismantling of Chelsea in the previous round will give them high hopes of retaining the title

programme.desc: Homer finds himself caught in the throes of a midlife crisis, so he takes up bass guitar and forms a band with some of the other dads in Springfield. However, things get awkward when their singer - none other than Apu - proves so successful that he quickly outgrows the rest of the group, leading to jealousy and resentment

programme.title: Live UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Paris Saint-Germain v Liverpool (Kick-off 8.00pm). Peter Collins presents all the action from the quarter-final first-leg tie at Parc des Princes. These teams met in the last 16 stage last term and the Reds claimed a 1-0 win here thanks to a goal by substitute Harvey Elliott, only for PSG to prevail by the same scoreline at Anfield and prevail on penalties. The French outfit went on to lift the trophy, and their 8-2 aggregate dismantling of Chelsea in the previous round will give them high hopes of retaining the title

programme.title: Greys Anatomy
programme.sub-title: (If You Want It) Do It Yourself
programme.desc: Teddy and Winston clash over a case while Owen and Jules treat a urologist, and Lucas struggles to prioritise patients. Drama, starring Kim Raver and Anthony Hill

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr consider the kitchen. With a love for eating and entertaining, its crucial to the project that they get this social space perfect. They plan a Spanish-tiled extravaganza, but are initially unable to agree on a colour scheme. Alans sloppy bricklaying skills are put to the test when Scott ropes him in to try to build a traditional cooker hood, but Amanda is unimpressed with the result. The best friends head to Seville for a tapas tour at the citys gastronomic hub. Though not a fan of heights, Alan prepares to face his fears when invited on a rock-climbing course

programme.title: Suspect
programme.desc: Bruised by his encounter with Nicola, Danny goes to the Crimson Orchid gentlemens club to look for Maia, who Nicola has accused of corrupting Christina. Maia stonewalls Dannys questions, but when he tells her that Christina may have been murdered, her façade of indifference cracks. Danny knows that Christina was involved in criminal activity, and he assumes Maia has lured her into prostitution, but in the face of her angry denials, he realises that Christina was selling drugs

programme.title: The Change
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Bridget Christie. Fifty-year-old mother of two Linda is told that what she thought was early onset dementia is actually menopause. So she decides to do something for herself for a change and reconnect with the person that she used to be

programme.title: EuroNews
programme.desc: European and international headlines live via satellite

programme.title: Garbo: Where Did You Go?
programme.desc: A portrait of Hollywood icon Greta Garbo, exploring her fame, famous isolation and her decision to retire from acting. This documentary includes previously unseen archive footage, letters from Garbo, and re-enactments of her life. Narrated by Noomi Rapace

programme.title: Catching Fire: The Story of Anita Pallenberg (2023)
programme.desc: Documentary about the Italian actress and artist, who was known as the muse of the Rolling Stones, based on an autobiographical manuscript discovered by her children after her death in 2017. With readings by Scarlett Johansson

Starring Ian McShane as ruthless saloon owner Al Swearengen, Timothy Olyphant as store manager Seth Bullock and Molly Parker as Alma Garrett

Starring Ian McShane as ruthless saloon owner Al Swearengen, Timothy Olyphant as store manager Seth Bullock and Molly Parker as Alma Garrett

programme.desc: Richard E Grant travels to Venice, said to be the city with the highest concentration of luxury hotels on the planet. programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade (1989)
programme.desc: The intrepid archaeologist sets out to find his estranged father, who has gone missing while searching for the Holy Grail. Once reunited, the duo team up to finish the task and locate the sacred relic before the Nazis get their hands on it. Action adventure sequel directed by Steven Spielberg, the third instalment in the successful franchise. Harrison Ford stars, alongside Sean Connery, Alison Doody, River Phoenix, Julian Glover and Denholm Elliott

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark (1981)
programme.desc: Intrepid archaeologist Indiana Jones goes in search of the legendary Ark of the Covenant, a biblical artefact of devastating power. His quest leads him to Egypt, where the Nazis are plotting to use the Ark to further Hitlers plans for world domination - aided by Joness deceitful French rival. The first of five big-screen action adventures, starring Harrison Ford as Indy, with Karen Allen, Paul Freeman and John Rhys-Davies

programme.title: The Mummy (1999)
programme.desc: A soldier of fortune joins an academic and her conniving brother on an expedition to an ancient Egyptian lost city. The explorers accidentally cause the spirit of a vengeful priest with awe-inspiring supernatural powers to rise from his tomb, leading to a desperate battle for survival. Fantasy action adventure, starring Brendan Fraser, Rachel Weisz, John Hannah, Arnold Vosloo and Oded Fehr

programme.title: The Golden Compass (2007)
programme.desc: Orphan Lyra Belacqua lives in a parallel universe populated by magical and menacing characters. When her best friend becomes the latest in a long line of children to mysteriously vanish, she embarks on a perilous mission to uncover the dark secret behind their disappearance. Fantasy adventure based on Philip Pullmans novel Northern Lights, starring Dakota Blue Richards, Nicole Kidman, Daniel Craig and Eva Green. Featuring the voices of Ian McKellen, Kristin Scott Thomas and Kathy Bates

Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover

Family adventure directed by Robert Rodriguez, starring Antonio Banderas, Carla Gugino, Alan Cumming, Alexa Vega and Daryl Sabara

Family adventure sequel, starring Antonio Banderas, Steve Buscemi and Carla Gugino

Writer/director Seth Worleys fantasy comedy, starring Tony Hale and DArcy Carden

Writer/director Jim Owens comedy thriller, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Tanya Moodie and Nicholas Asbury

programme.desc: Failed actress Lucy Mason is a star employee of the Adore matchmaking agency. She prefers the life of a singleton after her previous relationship with actor John imploded because of his precarious finances. At a clients wedding, Lucy meets the grooms brother, Harry, and politely rebuffs his amorous advances, suggesting he should enrol with Adore. Harry persists and Lucy agrees to go on a date, on condition that he signs up with the agency after their lunchtime liaison. Writer/director Celine Songs romantic comedy, starring Dakota Johnson, Chris Evans and Pedro Pascal

programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring African-American representation in Hollywood and TV, showcasing the creators and artists who shaped it. programme.title: Amy (2015)
programme.desc: Documentary portrait of soul, jazz and RnB singer-songwriter Amy Winehouse, whose untimely death in July 2011 followed a well-publicised history of drugs and alcohol abuse. Director Asif Kapadia traces Amys rise and demise over the 13 years preceding her death, telling her story via her music and autobiographical song lyrics, video footage shot by friends and family, archive television clips, plus voiceover interviews with those who were personally and professionally close to her. Contributors include Pete Doherty, Mitch Winehouse, Tony Bennett, Mark Ronson and Tyler James

programme.title: I Am Patrick Swayze
programme.desc: A tribute to the American actor, known for his roles in hits like Dirty Dancing, Ghost and The Outsiders. With contributions by Demi Moore and Rob Lowe among others

programme.title: Alien Disclosure Files
programme.desc: Millions of years ago Mars, aka the Red Planet, had a lot in common with Earth, but its now a desert wasteland. Do the doorways recently discovered on the planets surface lead to an existential lesson for mankind?

programme.title: Alien Disclosure Files
programme.desc: According to physics, time travel is possible. Could it be that Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena arent coming from somewhere else, but from here, though at a different time?

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy McGrath heads to the home of palaeontology on Dorsets Jurassic Coast and braves the collapsing cliffs of Lyme Regis to bring back one of its many fossils. While in the county, he investigates UFO-infested hillforts, haunted castles, chalk giants and shares a song with the fairies at the Singing Barrows of Weymouth

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Matsalu Moose: Wild Giants of the Baltics
programme.desc: Matsalu National Park at the Baltic Sea is known for being one of Europes most important stopover places for migrating birds. However, few know that an exceptionally large moose colony also lives here. This documentary follows the moose through the eyes of two yearlings as they are chased away from their mother before she gives birth to the next calf

programme.title: Kingdom of the Tide
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at the intertidal zone, the point at which the ocean meets the land, and the struggle for survival facing the creatures that inhabit this realm. Their success is critical, because this is one of the most important habitats on the planet - a vital connection between worlds

programme.title: Great Lakes Wild

programme.title: Bad Boys II (2003)
programme.desc: Two mismatched undercover cops set out to halt an underground gang war and bring down a drug lord attempting to flood the market with a deadly form of Ecstasy. However, a romantic interlude threatens to drive the investigation off course. Comedy thriller sequel, starring Will Smith, Martin Lawrence, Gabrielle Union, Jordi Molla and Peter Stormare

programme.title: Mad Max (1979)
programme.desc: In the near future, Australian cop Max Rockatansky tires of battling to impose law and order on the roads and turns in his badge - only to be forced out of retirement when his wife and child are murdered at the hands of a motorcycle gang. George Millers action thriller, starring Mel Gibson, Joanne Samuel and Hugh Keays-Byrne

programme.title: Mad Max 2 (1981)
programme.desc: A former cop haunted by his tragic past roams the wasteland of Australia in the aftermath of a nuclear war. He arrives at a besieged settlement and grudgingly helps protect the residents from a horde of marauding bikers who want to get hold of their valuable supply of petrol. Futuristic adventure sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Bruce Spence, Vernon Wells, Emil Minty and Kjell Nilsson

programme.title: Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome (1985)
programme.desc: The nomadic warrior pitches up in the city of a cut-throat desert community in search of his stolen vehicle and ends up making a deal with a local dictator to get rid of her rival for power. However, when the bargain turns sour, he is cast out into the wilderness - where he becomes the unlikely protector of a tribe of feral youngsters. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Tina Turner and Bruce Spence

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga (2024)
programme.desc: In a post-apocalyptic world, a kidnapped girl grows into a warrior who seeks vengeance against a great biker horde led by the crazed Dementus. Director George Millers Mad Max: Fury Road prequel, starring Anya Taylor-Joy, Chris Hemsworth and Tom Burke

programme.title: Back to the Future (1985)
programme.desc: Teenager Marty McFly travels back to the 1950s in a time machine built by his eccentric inventor friend. The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mothers heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover

programme.title: Back to the Future Part II (1989)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly travels to the 21st century, where he has to save his children from a terrible fate. However, this proves to have unforeseen consequences when the future version of arch enemy Biff sees a chance to rewrite history for his own benefit, leaving Marty stuck in a twisted parallel universe. Sci-fi comedy sequel, starring Michael J Fox and Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: Back to the Future Part III (1990)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly is transported back to the days of the Wild West, where he strives to save his eccentric scientist friend Doc Brown from death at the hands of a villainous gunslinger. Final instalment in the sci-fi comedy trilogy, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Mary Steenburgen and Thomas F Wilson

programme.title: Venom (2018)
programme.desc: While exploring space for new worlds, a probe belonging to the Life Foundation discovers a comet covered in symbiotic lifeforms. Following the probes return to Earth, journalist Eddie Brock investigates sinister trials being carried out by the corporation, and after being infected by an alien symbiote, develops superhuman strength and power. Fantasy adventure, starring Tom Hardy and Riz Ahmed

programme.title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage (2021)
programme.desc: Struggling to cope with his alter ego Venom, Eddie Brock attempts to re-ignite his journalistic career by interviewing serial killer Cletus Kasady. However, Kasady becomes the host of the symbiote Carnage and escapes prison after a failed execution. Comic-book fantasy sequel, directed by Andy Serkis and starring Tom Hardy, Woody Harrelson and Michelle Williams

programme.title: Venom: The Last Dance (2024)
programme.desc: Journalist Eddie Brock and symbiote partner-in-crime Venom are lying low in Mexico City, on the run for the apparent murder of detective Patrick Mulligan. The cop and his bonded entity, Toxin, are actually alive at the Imperium symbiote containment programme, beneath the Area 51 US Air Force facility in the Nevada desert. Knull, the symbiotes immortal creator, dispatches hunters to locate Venoms hidden power source, the Codex, which can set Knull free to terrorise the multiverse. Fantasy adventure, starring Tom Hardy, Chiwetel Ejiofor, Stephen Graham and Juno Temple

programme.title: Annabelle (2014)
programme.desc: A man buys his pregnant wife a vintage doll, which places their lives in jeopardy. Members of a Satanic cult have their eyes on the antique toy, and break into their house to prepare a ritual that will transform the harmless plaything into a terrifying monster. Horror spin-off from The Conjuring, starring Annabelle Wallis and Ward Horton

programme.title: Jurassic World (2015)
programme.desc: Decades on from the creation of Jurassic Park, a fully operational attraction filled with genetically engineered dinosaurs has been opened on the island. In need of a new attraction, the company behind the park creates an entirely new species - only for it to escape and terrorise the tourists. Action adventure sequel, starring Chris Pratt and Bryce Dallas Howard

programme.title: Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom (2018)
programme.desc: Three years after the destruction of Jurassic World, Owen Grady and Claire Dearing are asked to return to the theme park to save its resident dinosaurs from the islands active volcano. During their mission, they uncover a conspiracy that may affect the entire planet. Action sequel, starring Chris Pratt, Bryce Dallas Howard, Rafe Spall and Toby Jones

programme.title: Jurassic World: Rebirth (2025)
programme.desc: Profit-hungry pharmaceutical representative Martin Krebs contracts covert operations specialist Zora Bennett to assemble a crack team to infiltrate a forbidden island and collect the DNA of three key dinosaur species. Fantasy adventure, starring Scarlett Johansson, Mahershala Ali and Jonathan Bailey

programme.title: Annabelle: Creation (2017)
programme.desc: Twelve years after the death of their little girl, a dollmaker and his wife welcome a nun and several girls from an orphanage into their home. There they become the target of the craftsmans possessed creation, Annabelle. Horror, starring Anthony LaPaglia, Samara Lee and Miranda Otto.

programme.title: Warehouse 13
programme.desc: A spate of deaths occurs in which women turn to clay, and DNA tests suggest a computer virus is the cause. Pete and Myka try to fathom why they have woken up in bed together

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Carl ends up fighting for his life after being accidentally shot by a ranch hand hunting for deer, leading the group to a farm owned by a doctor who sets about saving the boy. Rick donates blood to keep his son alive, while Shane and the hunter go in search of urgent medical supplies at a nearby school - only to find it overrun with walkers

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: The group anxiously waits for Shane to return, unaware he is trapped inside a derelict school surrounded by the undead - and with every passing minute, Carl grows weaker, leaving Rick and Lori facing the agonising decision of whether to let Hershel operate without the necessary medical equipment. Meanwhile, Daryl and Andrea search for Sophia in the woods. Post-apocalyptic drama, starring Jon Bernthal, Andrew Lincoln and Sarah Wayne Callies

programme.title: Most Haunted

programme.desc: A brother-and-sister team of intergalactic environmentalists resort to desperate measures to prove Starfleets warp drives are harming the universe. Sci-fi drama, starring Patrick Stewart

programme.title: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
programme.desc: A battle-injured Nog seeks refuge in the holosuite, hoping the virtual world of Bashirs 1962 Las Vegas lounge will provide the comfort he needs. Starring Aron Eisenberg and Avery Brooks

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: ONeill and Tealc are exposed to a strange alien device and find themselves caught in a temporal anomaly. Faced with repeatedly living the same day over again they must convince everyone else about what is happening and find a way to break the time loop. Sci-fi adventure, with Richard Dean Anderson and Christopher Judge

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Hank and Nick investigate an attack on a farmers horses which puts them on the trail of the legendary Bigfoot

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: The latest case offers Nick the chance to discover how his parents were murdered as Hank tries to come to terms with the existence of the supernatural

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: An angry Shane tells Rick they are wasting their time looking for Sophia, while Daryl finds the missing girls doll, only to be thrown from his horse and injured, leaving him open to attack from walkers. Hershel grows tired of the disputes within the group and makes it clear they cannot stay with him indefinitely, and Glenn discovers a horrible secret in the barn

programme.title: Warehouse 13
programme.desc: Jinks and Claudia are hit by poison darts while out to collect an artefact, and are awoken by a strange dog. Myka relentlessly pursues her former partners murderer

programme.title: FBI
programme.desc: When an undercover FBI agent is killed in the field, the team makes the unnerving discovery that the identities of multiple undercover agents have been compromised, including Maggie who has been actively working to infiltrate an eco-terrorist group. programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: A pupil at a school for troubled youngsters is found beaten to death in a forest. A hand-drawn map discovered in a dormitory leads Kimball and Wayne to a run-down shack where they find a volatile man who has been harassed by a secretive gang of children hurling paint at his property, shouting abuse and breaking his windows. Suspecting the group could be behind the boys death, Patrick sets up an elaborate sting involving a campfire, a ghost story and a willing volunteer. Simon Baker stars

programme.desc: The comedian returns with the programme in which he tries to make sense of the modern world, using a combination of stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction to dissect the weeks biggest issues - with Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and a panel of guests on hand to take Frankie to task over his claims

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: David and Patrick throw an open mic night at the store. Johnny and Alexis begin to rebrand the motel, but Stevie fears they are over-expanding

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: The Rose family throws a barbecue to celebrate David and Patricks four-month anniversary, but an unexpected visitor threatens to destroy their relationship. Alexis struggles to find meaning when she gets a random text from Ted

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Johnny is warmly embraced by the Jazzagals, David and Stevie take a brief vacation, and Alexis goes out with Teds rival

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the British tradition of cooking with food hunted from the land and prepare a selection of dishes using rabbit, grouse and venison

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Police in Surrey are on the case of a violent gang who steal millions of pounds worth of jewellery, and Avon and Somerset police tackle guns being sent through the post from Miami.

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: The National Crime Agency - Britains answer to the FBI - tackle a people-smuggling ring, a Ghanaian cocaine smuggling operation, and organised online romance fraud

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: In Perth, the pressure is on for a team tackling a high-stakes engine rebuild, and in Townsville, a crew of experts build a massive underframe for a freight powerhouse

programme.title: Rick Steins Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef continues down the Californian coastline immortalised by John Steinbeck, a region of citrus groves and vineyards. On the edge of Los Angeles, Rick discovers Olvera Street, where the Hispanic origins of the city took root. Then heading south to San Diego, he meets 79-year-old Peter Halmay, the oldest sea-urchin diver in town, and is served the best fish chilli he has ever had by a local fishmonger

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers hand over a convict to Mexican authorities in the middle of the Juarez Lincoln bridge, and a man drops a marijuana joint on a customs floor in Progreso

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A man fears for his life after being stopped by a cartel in Laredo, clubbers in Detroit are found with drugs disguised in liquid, and officers stop a shipment of frozen rats

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy Martin helps to restore Birmingham Botanical Gardens to its former glory and tries to learn how to blow glass to use in the enormous greenhouses. He tries to rebuild one of the first lawnmowers and uses a little-known 19th-century technique to make his mark on the rockery, before he is given the job of turning on his newly installed fountain at the grand opening ceremony

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Kia Ceed through a rigorous but peculiar road test featuring eels, curry, a rock legend, an American police officer and an unusual game of rugby involving James May. Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the Mastretta MXT, but worries about the repercussions of a comment he made in a previous episode about Mexico, the sports cars country of manufacture. Plus, three high-performance hatchbacks are driven with gusto around the test track, and Formula One driver Lewis Hamilton gets a second chance to smash the lap record

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond try to solve the problems that increasingly complex cars pose to an ageing population. They design a vehicle for elderly people and put it to the test in Dorset with the help of three pensioners. Meanwhile, James May gets acquainted with a revamped Range Rover in London before pitting the luxurious off-roader against an autonomous military machine in Nevada. Actor James McAvoy tackles the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how certain British ingredients make traditional combinations, preparing a pork and apple dish and a pear and Stilton tart. They also find out how Yorkshire pudding was made in the 1700s, and discover it was not originally served with beef

programme.title: Rick Steins Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef heads across one of the busiest border crossings in the world - San Diego to Tijuana - for the start of his Mexican adventures. He follows the Pacific coastline down to Ensenada, and samples flour tortilla burritos, fresh fish, marinated and cooked on Popotla Beach, and visits the Valle de Guadalupe - Mexicos best kept secret for fabulous wines

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: More material from the comedy panel show, not originally shown in the series 17 episodes. Hosted by Rob Brydon, with team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack joined by celebrity guests including Charlie Brooker, Johnny Marr, Shazia Mirza, Craig Charles, Kimberley Walsh, Frankie Boyle, Will Mellor, Jeremy Vine, Danny Jones, Charlene White, Amy Gledhill, Jessica Knappett, Sam Campbell and Mo Gilligan

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Maisie Adam, Rory Bremner, James Cracknell and Michelle Gayle go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Charlie Brooks, Les Dennis, Darren Harriott and Melinda Messenger go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Gary Neville hosts an extended episode of the current affairs quiz, with comedian Maisie Adam and presenter Richard Madeley joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop

programme.title: Frankie Boyles New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian tackles the biggest issues troubling the planet, making a string of bold and often outrageous statements which hell then pick apart with the help of regular contributors Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and a variety of other guests, including comics and experts

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Host Sandi Toksvig is joined by panellists Bridget Christie, Rosie Jones, Cariad Lloyd and Alan Davies. They explore Ulex and other uncommon U words

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara OBriain, Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons are joined by Josh Widdicombe, Katherine Ryan, Milton Jones and Ed Gamble to give their irreverent takes on the biggest stories to hit the headlines, through stand-up spots and improvised games. First aired in September 2015

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenters Alex Jones and Rick Edwards, comedian Greg Davies and performance poet John Cooper Clarke join forces with team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the panel show in which participants attempt to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts an extended edition of the quiz with a difference, beginning the K series with a range of fiendish questions on such topics as knees and knockers. Regular panellist Alan Davies is joined by Sara Pascoe, Jack Whitehall and David Mitchell, with points being awarded for interesting answers as well as correct ones

programme.title: Frankie Boyles New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian tackles the biggest issues troubling the planet, making a string of bold and often outrageous statements which hell then pick apart with the help of regular contributors Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and a variety of other guests, including comics and experts

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: David realises that his time apart from Patrick has gone on for too long and he looks for a way to reconcile. Moira pitches a Singles Week event at the Town Hall. Sitcom, starring Eugene Levy

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Jocelyn bullies David into throwing her a last-minute baby shower, Alexis runs into an old friend, and Moira and Johnny do field research for the towns upcoming singles week. Sitcom, starring Eugene Levy

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Moira tries to get out of jury duty and David attempts damage control for a Rose Apothecary product

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history behind some of the countrys most popular spices and explore how they have been used to liven up meals. The culinary duo also visit a chilli festival - with mouth-numbing consequences

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: A Surrey police response unit is called to a stabbing incident at a teen house party in a captivating insight into teamwork, tactics and compassion

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: The National Crime Agency take on a people-trafficking group while Surrey and City of London Police hunt down exploitative romance fraudsters

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A locomotive needs a quick double bogie change to get back to the sugarcane harvest. Meanwhile, the heat is on for a tricky air conditioner replacement

programme.title: Rick Steins Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef heads across one of the busiest border crossings in the world - San Diego to Tijuana - for the start of his Mexican adventures. He follows the Pacific coastline down to Ensenada, and samples flour tortilla burritos, fresh fish, marinated and cooked on Popotla Beach, and visits the Valle de Guadalupe - Mexicos best kept secret for fabulous wines

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Border officers in Detroit apprehend a woman after finding a handgun and ammunition in her vehicle, and officers in Eagle Pass find $150,000-worth of methamphetamine

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers find a manmade compartment within the trailer of a truck returning Stateside, and two Americans arouse suspicions with an unsafe lane change at the US-Canada border

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer and lorry mechanic attempts, with the aid of slipstreaming, to break the British record for outright speed on a bicycle - 110mph. Guy recruits a team comprising a truck racer, a bike builder and a design engineer to prepare for the feat, and undergoes a training regime with double Olympic cycling gold medallist Laura Trott before heading to Pendine Sands on the shores of Carmarthen Bay for his challenge

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May head to New Zealand to take part in a race along the coastline to the tip of the North Island - with one behind the wheel of a car and the other in an Americas Cup yacht. Back in the UK, Richard Hammond tries out three hot hatches as he puts the Renaultsport Clio 200, Peugeot 208 GTi and Ford Fiesta ST through their paces, and a host of stars including Warwick Davis, Charles Dance and Joss Stone assemble at the test track to welcome a new Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of the Ferrari F12 Berlinetta, sampling the punch of its 730bhp V12 engine on the open roads of Scotland. Richard Hammond harnesses the power of motorsport on a quest to find the worlds best taxi, and James May pays tribute to the former BBC Television Centre in White City, London, using a motorcyclist and a pair of parkour runners. Plus, the BAC Mono - an astonishingly fast car with just one seat - is put through its paces on the test track, and film director Ron Howard goes for a spin in the new Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how Britains love of cheese has evolved over centuries, travelling to Scotland to sample some of its finest varieties and discovering that the original cheesecake recipes were not American but British

programme.title: Rick Steins Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef lands in the town that placed Tequila on the map and explores Guadalajara, the city that gave the rest of the world Mariachis and dishes like carne con chilli. He also cant resist a visit to Casa Kimberly, bought by Richard Burton for Elizabeth Taylor, and where they still serve her favourite cocktail, a chocolate martini

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon introduces a seasonal special of the comedy panel game. programme.title: Rick Steins Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef continues down the Californian coastline immortalised by John Steinbeck, a region of citrus groves and vineyards. On the edge of Los Angeles, Rick discovers Olvera Street, where the Hispanic origins of the city took root. Then heading south to San Diego, he meets 79-year-old Peter Halmay, the oldest sea-urchin diver in town, and is served the best fish chilli he has ever had by a local fishmonger

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers hand over a convict to Mexican authorities in the middle of the Juarez Lincoln bridge, and a man drops a marijuana joint on a customs floor in Progreso

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A man fears for his life after being stopped by a cartel in Laredo, clubbers in Detroit are found with drugs disguised in liquid, and officers stop a shipment of frozen rats

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy Martin helps to restore Birmingham Botanical Gardens to its former glory and tries to learn how to blow glass to use in the enormous greenhouses. He tries to rebuild one of the first lawnmowers and uses a little-known 19th-century technique to make his mark on the rockery, before he is given the job of turning on his newly installed fountain at the grand opening ceremony

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Kia Ceed through a rigorous but peculiar road test featuring eels, curry, a rock legend, an American police officer and an unusual game of rugby involving James May. Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the Mastretta MXT, but worries about the repercussions of a comment he made in a previous episode about Mexico, the sports cars country of manufacture. Plus, three high-performance hatchbacks are driven with gusto around the test track, and Formula One driver Lewis Hamilton gets a second chance to smash the lap record

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond try to solve the problems that increasingly complex cars pose to an ageing population. They design a vehicle for elderly people and put it to the test in Dorset with the help of three pensioners. Meanwhile, James May gets acquainted with a revamped Range Rover in London before pitting the luxurious off-roader against an autonomous military machine in Nevada. Actor James McAvoy tackles the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how certain British ingredients make traditional combinations, preparing a pork and apple dish and a pear and Stilton tart. They also find out how Yorkshire pudding was made in the 1700s, and discover it was not originally served with beef

programme.title: Rick Steins Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef heads across one of the busiest border crossings in the world - San Diego to Tijuana - for the start of his Mexican adventures. programme.desc: Compo tracks down his missing ferret to Noras washing basket, but smuggling it out unnoticed proves tricky. Bill Owen and Kathy Staff star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Sid attempts to install a doorbell in his cafe, so Compo, Clegg and Foggy try to assist - with disastrous consequences. John Comer, Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde and Bill Owen star in Roy Clarkes long-running comedy

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest spies trouble when Lady Felicia returns to Kembleford from Africa, having split from her lover Benedict Northam. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: A skeleton found buried in Edie Paigntons allotment is difficult for Sister Boniface to identify as male or female. Paigntons husband has disappeared, as has Frank Harsdens pregnant daughter Jenny. More tests by Sister Boniface reveal the victim had been murdered with a cultivator and buried in a caustic soda mixture.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a 1970s guitarist is investigated following the deathbed confession of a witness. Strickland, a fan of the rock stars band, reveals himself to be a keen musician, while Brian tracks down Jack and persuades him to rejoin Ucos. With guest appearances by Roger Lloyd Pack and George Costigan

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective struggles to cope following the death of his wife and suffers more grief when the body of his godson is discovered in a bricked-up drain 12 years after he went missing. Lynley himself becomes a suspect when the boys sister is found dead, forcing him to enlist the help of his lawyer and DS McCaffreys son to prove his innocence. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty and amiable sidekick Geoffrey become embroiled in a convoluted case involving a deaf birdwatcher who goes missing at the same time as a womans body is found at the bottom of a cliff he is known to frequent. Light-hearted drama, with Patricia Routledge and Dominic Monaghan, featuring a guest appearance by David Bower

programme.title: Whitstable Pearl
programme.desc: A retired policeman is murdered during a Civil War re-enactment and the increasingly complicated investigation into his death uncovers three mistresses and a 35-year-old feud

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Top television spy series Operation QT is filming at the convent when a real bullet is fired instead of a replica, narrowly missing the leading actor. Working with DS Livingstone, Sister Boniface soon works out the real target was the shows producer, Dick Lansky, who has many enemies on and off set and a roving eye for the actresses. Lorna Watson stars

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how George IIIs mental illness changed Britain, uncovering royal papers and exploring how the attempt on the kings life by a mentally ill subject transformed psychiatry forever

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry acts as Junes defence in court after she is involved in an incident with a corporation dustcart. However, his key witness turns out to be less than reliable in recollecting the facts

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team reveals why the Isle of Harris is the perfect location for road-testing cabriolets, and find out if it is possible to parachute into a moving car travelling at 50mph. Richard Hammond unveils the blueprint for the perfect roadster and Ranulph Fiennes is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines four dramatic chapters in British history, exploring how changing attitudes to gender politics, class inequality, mental health and children can challenge perceptions of the past and provide new answers to each mystery. She begins with the 16th-century witch hunts, when thousands of ordinary people, mostly women, were hunted down, tortured and killed across Scotland and England

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines Britains deadliest pandemic, exploring how its monumental death toll changed the face of the country for ever and what the sudden loss of life meant for the church and religious beliefs, landowners and for the those who survived. Lucy also learns how DNA extracted from the skeletons of the plagues victims enabled scientists to finally identify a bacteria called Yersinia Pestis - a pathogen to which the mediaeval population had no immunity

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy uncovers the story of Edward IVs sons Edward and Richard, whose disappearance in 1483 has led to centuries of mystery. The princes were taken to the Tower of London for their own protection, but a priest declared the boys illegitimate and their uncle Richard next in line to the throne. The gaps in the historical record have fuelled 500 years of speculation, so Lucy speaks to history professor Tim Thornton, who has found evidence of one account of what happened written by Thomas More, while Richard III Society chair Matthew Lewis gives his views on the events

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how George IIIs mental illness changed Britain, uncovering royal papers and exploring how the attempt on the kings life by a mentally ill subject transformed psychiatry forever

programme.desc: The historian examines four dramatic chapters in British history, exploring how changing attitudes to gender politics, class inequality, mental health and children can challenge perceptions of the past and provide new answers to each mystery. She begins with the 16th-century witch hunts, when thousands of ordinary people, mostly women, were hunted down, tortured and killed across Scotland and England

Lucy also learns how DNA extracted from the skeletons of the plagues victims enabled scientists to finally identify a bacteria called Yersinia Pestis - a pathogen to which the mediaeval population had no immunity

programme.desc: Part five of seven. The Brigadier summons the Doctor and Liz to his research centre after several of his staff suffer nervous breakdowns

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part six of seven. While the Doctor and Liz try to find a cure to the virus spread by Baker, the young Silurian seizes control of his people.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how George IIIs mental illness changed Britain, uncovering royal papers and exploring how the attempt on the kings life by a mentally ill subject transformed psychiatry forever

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines four dramatic chapters in British history, exploring how changing attitudes to gender politics, class inequality, mental health and children can challenge perceptions of the past and provide new answers to each mystery. She begins with the 16th-century witch hunts, when thousands of ordinary people, mostly women, were hunted down, tortured and killed across Scotland and England

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Lindisfarne, which lies off the Northumberland coast. From what became known as the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, monks spread Christianity to the rest of northern England and created one of Britains finest treasures - the Lindisfarne Gospels. Viking attacks caused the monks to flee but the village survived and today the community has to share it with thousands of tourists

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Man and Beast with Martin Clunes
programme.desc: The comedy actor sets out on an international journey to investigate the extraordinary relationship between man and beast. In the first of two episodes, Martin travels to Nepal, where he meets a farmer whose cows not only supply his income from milk, but also provide the fuel for lighting and cooking in the family home

programme.title: Man and Beast with Martin Clunes
programme.desc: The actor concludes his international exploration into the relationship between man and beast. In the second of two programmes, he meets doting dog owners in Tokyo, learns the truth behind the outlawed practice of snake-charming in Nepal and find out how small birds in Thailand are used for gambling. Back in Britain, he partakes in a traditional swan-upping event on the River Thames to monitor the population of the birds, and discovers how horses are used to help autistic children

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: With more than 8,000 islands to choose from, Martin Clunes visits 16 which provide an insight into the diversity, history and challenges of island life in Australia. His first stop is Lord Howe Island, where the pace of life is slow and the population is a steady 350. He also visits Norfolk Island, Australias most easterly territory, and the remotest island on Martins journey

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of Australia
programme.desc: The actor travels to the Tiwi Islands, swims with a whale shark, and visits the Houtman Abrolhos, a string of over 100 little islands. Martin Clunes also samples life on Rottnest Island, which has long been a playground retreat for mainland visitors

programme.title: Man and Beast with Martin Clunes

programme.title: Doctor Who

programme.title: Doctor Who

programme.desc: Part one of seven. The Doctor investigates a research centre where monsters have been sighted in nearby caves. Guest starring Fulton Mackay (Porridge) and Paul Darrow (Blakes 7)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of seven. The Doctor leads an expedition into the caves in search of the creatures. Captain Hawkins fires on a mysterious humanoid figure, who flees to the surface

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part three of seven. The Silurians contact Quinn for help retrieving the cohort, and the Doctor and the Brigadier organise a search of the moors.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part four of seven. When Quinn turns up dead, the Brigadier leads an armed party into the caves. Meanwhile, the Doctor tries to reach out to the Silurians

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part five of seven. The Brigadier summons the Doctor and Liz to his research centre after several of his staff suffer nervous breakdowns

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part six of seven. While the Doctor and Liz try to find a cure to the virus spread by Baker, the young Silurian seizes control of his people.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part seven of seven. While Liz neutralises the threat of the virus, the Doctor learns of an even more devastating Silurian plan.

programme.title: Man and Beast with Martin Clunes
programme.desc: The comedy actor sets out on an international journey to investigate the extraordinary relationship between man and beast. In the first of two episodes, Martin travels to Nepal, where he meets a farmer whose cows not only supply his income from milk, but also provide the fuel for lighting and cooking in the family home

programme.title: Harry Enfields Television Programme
programme.desc: Comedy sketches with the multi-faceted funnyman and his partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke. Featuring characters Wayne and Waynetta Slob, Tim Nice-But-Dim, The Scousers and DJs Smashie and Nicey

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee imagines having the perfect life with Lucy, but the reality may not be good enough to match up to his dreams. Comedy, guest starring Bobby Ball, with Lee Mack, Tim Vine, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy announces that she plans to marry Pavlov, a mechanic from the old Eastern bloc who needs her help to stay in the country and avoid returning to his tragic life back home. However, Lee is the only one who seems to object to the arrangement. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Tim Vine and Timothy West

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lucy needs help writing an important speech she is due to give at a recruitment conference, Lee hopes to impress her by offering his services. However, Tim also thinks he could do a good job and it turns into a fierce competition to discover who can come up with the wittiest lines. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Tim Vine

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: Agnes tries to make friends with Dermots mother-in-law Hillary ahead of the babys birth, and suggests they go out to dinner together. However, the evening comes to an abrupt end for the two feuding matriarchs when expectant mum Maria goes into labour, and the Browns are forced to rush her to hospital. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplins plans for expansion cause chaos with the Yellowcoats when an old cottage is scheduled for demolition. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie decides his staff need to work harder and vows to sack the next employee he catches slacking. Mrs Cakeworthy is the first out the door, but she soon has her boss regretting his decision when he learns she is a witch. John Challis, Sue Holderness and Ella Kenion star

programme.desc: The inmates decide on a wager as the parole board gathers, with Blanco odds-on favourite for release. However, the old lag turns down his freedom and leaves Fletcher in danger of losing a small fortune. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Beth and Tyler announce they have become vegetarians and set off to Ludlow races to protest about the treatment of animals. Meanwhile, Jeds scarecrow in the barn is mistaken for a human body, and Marlene enrols on a mail-order reiki healing course as part of a plan to create a massage parlour and beauty salon at the farm

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The staff wonder who will benefit from an opportunity for promotion to the board, and Mr Humphries reveals his clairvoyant abilities. Shop-floor comedy, with John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry becomes dejected when his fitness-mad neighbour Boothroyd begins to outshine him in all things physical. However, he discovers that when it comes to karate, he may just have an advantage over his new rival. Long-running comedy, guest starring Brian Conley

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Will the Genuine Racer Please Stand Up?
programme.desc: Howard becomes ill at ease with his lot in life, and tries to change his appearance so that not even Pearl would recognise him. But he soon discovers that the drawbacks of assuming someone elses identity are greater than the benefits

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and the team head to the Worcestershire village of Eckington to help 17-year-old Antonia Payne-Cheney, who has Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, a rare and debilitating condition that causes her joints to regularly dislocate and means she cannot eat normally and is fed through a tube. The ambitious build features a functional, sterile space for medical procedures in her bedroom, a specially adapted wet room and an accessible area where she can socialise with friends and family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: When Terry Guest had a brain injury, he was left with severe disabilities, and the only care available for him was an old peoples dementia care home, where he remained for several years. His sister Tracey offered help, but like Terrys home, her house was also unsuitable. Nick Knowless regulars and a team of South Yorkshire volunteers and suppliers step in to adapt Traceys home and build Terry his own separate pad at the end of the garden

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Bidford-on-Avon, Warwickshire, to meet Chloe, who has a rare genetic disorder that means she has to stay in the living room of her familys house under constant care. The DIY SOS team are joined by Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and hundreds of Warwickshire volunteers to redesign the entire house to provide Chloe with independence, creating a self-contained apartment complete with bedroom, wet room and sitting room

programme.title: The Supervet

Louis meets Rita at her dads pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her familys arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS at Great Ormond Street Hospital
programme.desc: The DIY SOS team are out of their comfort zone this week, joining forces with two world-famous institutions - Great Ormond Street Hospital and the Chelsea Flower Show - for a special challenge. Gardener Chris Beardshaw has promised his gold medal-winning garden from the flower show to the hospital - now all Nick Knowles and his fellow builders have to do is crane the display across London and rebuild it on the hospital. Its a hugely ambitious build, with tricky logistics and emotional volunteers. But knowing how much the garden will prove welcome respite for families, the team is determined not to fail

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, his team and a group of volunteers are in Monmouth, Wales, to transform the family home of Charlotte, a mother recovering from a stroke that left her with brain damage, speech loss and partial paralysis. The current layout of her home makes it difficult for Charlotte to move around and have her physiotherapy treatment, so the team redesign the house to make things easier for her, her husband Rob and their children

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to the Isle of Sheppey in Kent to meet two extraordinary dads, Garry and Kyle, who have adopted four vulnerable children - three of whom have special needs - to give them the best life possible. But their cramped three-bedroom bungalow is making family life difficult. So to give this family the space they so desperately need, the DIY SOS builders are undertaking their most ambitious project yet - to demolish the bungalow and build a bespoke five-bedroom house in its place. But with just nine days to complete the task, it looks like being their toughest one yet

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire bull terrier requires attention when it suffers complete paralysis in its back legs, and a Devon rex cat is brought in after being struck by a car. Its eight-year-old owner faces a tough decision when X-rays reveal a severely broken pelvis and a ruptured bladder. Plus, a young cavalier king has had seven operations in a bid to fix its knee joint, and Noel tries to resolve the problem

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries of veterinary medicine, and treats a German shepherd puppy that needs surgery on his hips and front leg using groundbreaking cell treatment. The vet must also perform delicate surgery on a chihuahua suffering from a neck deformity compressing its spinal cord - however, one false move could be fatal. Plus, a rescue dog with deformed front legs is brought into the surgery

Louis accompanies Katherine as she performs her latest stand-up tour, Missus, in Southend-On-Sea, and later, he spends a rare day off with Katherine, her daughter Violet and her husband Bobby

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Jo and Ash deal with a patient whose chronic cough leaves him struggling to breathe, while Joel and Nic treat a man whos fallen from a ladder while wielding a hedge trimmer

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing and TOWIE star Pete Wicks immerses himself in the heart-warming and emotional world of dog care at Basildons Dogs Trust rehoming centre. Pete returns to Dogs Trust, facing a suitcase of abandoned pups on day one and falling hard for Theo, a gentle Italian Mastiff with a serious heart condition.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and the team head to the Worcestershire village of Eckington to help 17-year-old Antonia Payne-Cheney, who has Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, a rare and debilitating condition that causes her joints to regularly dislocate and means she cannot eat normally and is fed through a tube. The ambitious build features a functional, sterile space for medical procedures in her bedroom, a specially adapted wet room and an accessible area where she can socialise with friends and family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: When Terry Guest had a brain injury, he was left with severe disabilities, and the only care available for him was an old peoples dementia care home, where he remained for several years. His sister Tracey offered help, but like Terrys home, her house was also unsuitable. Nick Knowless regulars and a team of South Yorkshire volunteers and suppliers step in to adapt Traceys home and build Terry his own separate pad at the end of the garden

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Bidford-on-Avon, Warwickshire, to meet Chloe, who has a rare genetic disorder that means she has to stay in the living room of her familys house under constant care. The DIY SOS team are joined by Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and hundreds of Warwickshire volunteers to redesign the entire house to provide Chloe with independence, creating a self-contained apartment complete with bedroom, wet room and sitting room

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Russian blue cat is brought into the surgery with a shattered jaw after a dog attack, and a man living in a homeless shelter brings in his best friend Spud, a Labrador cross with a lame back leg. Plus, Noel tries to help straighten a deformity in a six-month-old Chihuahua puppys leg

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Senior neurologist Dr Colin Driver comes up with an unconventional solution to treat a nurses cat, which has been rushed into the surgery after being poisoned. The owners of a rare Polish sheepdog with a failed fracture repair on a broken leg are faced with the option of having the leg removed, or trying an innovative total elbow replacement. Plus, a self-confessed crazy cat lady brings in a ginger tom which urgently needs leg surgery

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The work of NHS emergency response teams. Staff are scrambled to a block of flats after an explosion causes multiple casualties and a driver is trapped after his lorry hits a tree

programme.title: Butchers of the Bayou
programme.desc: Soon after Derrick Todd Lee was captured, new murders occurred. Evidence led to Sean Vincent Gillis, whose 2004 arrest revealed he was responsible for several brutal killings

programme.title: Butchers of the Bayou
programme.desc: A DNA sample linked Sean Vincent Gillis to the murders. Gillis and Derrick Todd Lee killed 20 women, and dozens of cases in Baton Rouge still remain unsolved

programme.title: Missing Persons Investigation
programme.desc: A look at the work police investigators and forensic specialists do across Australia to find missing people. Detectives in Sydney investigate the case of a young woman who has vanished in mysterious circumstances, while in Melbourne, police race to find a man missing during a storm

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Katie meets Candice, who is facing a second-degree murder charge, and learns that she was born into a gang and has grown up around violence

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Dougs fiancee wont move in until he clears his tiki collection and his dead ex-wifes things, so the Death Cleaners help him sort out the clutter in his heart and home

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dads pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her familys arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Today in Stoke, paramedics Mick and Rachel help a young man who has hurt his back. In Dudley, paramedics help a 74-year-old who had a shock from his internal defibrillator

programme.desc: Pete meets Lurcher Terri, whos terrified of the world and must learn to trust again, and helps settle a family of chihuahuas given up by their heartbroken owner

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS at Great Ormond Street Hospital
programme.desc: The DIY SOS team are out of their comfort zone this week, joining forces with two world-famous institutions - Great Ormond Street Hospital and the Chelsea Flower Show - for a special challenge. Gardener Chris Beardshaw has promised his gold medal-winning garden from the flower show to the hospital - now all Nick Knowles and his fellow builders have to do is crane the display across London and rebuild it on the hospital. Its a hugely ambitious build, with tricky logistics and emotional volunteers. But knowing how much the garden will prove welcome respite for families, the team is determined not to fail

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles, his team and a group of volunteers are in Monmouth, Wales, to transform the family home of Charlotte, a mother recovering from a stroke that left her with brain damage, speech loss and partial paralysis. The current layout of her home makes it difficult for Charlotte to move around and have her physiotherapy treatment, so the team redesign the house to make things easier for her, her husband Rob and their children

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to the Isle of Sheppey in Kent to meet two extraordinary dads, Garry and Kyle, who have adopted four vulnerable children - three of whom have special needs - to give them the best life possible. But their cramped three-bedroom bungalow is making family life difficult. So to give this family the space they so desperately need, the DIY SOS builders are undertaking their most ambitious project yet - to demolish the bungalow and build a bespoke five-bedroom house in its place. But with just nine days to complete the task, it looks like being their toughest one yet

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire bull terrier requires attention when it suffers complete paralysis in its back legs, and a Devon rex cat is brought in after being struck by a car. Its eight-year-old owner faces a tough decision when X-rays reveal a severely broken pelvis and a ruptured bladder. Plus, a young cavalier king has had seven operations in a bid to fix its knee joint, and Noel tries to resolve the problem

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries of veterinary medicine, and treats a German shepherd puppy that needs surgery on his hips and front leg using groundbreaking cell treatment. The vet must also perform delicate surgery on a chihuahua suffering from a neck deformity compressing its spinal cord - however, one false move could be fatal. Plus, a rescue dog with deformed front legs is brought into the surgery

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS at Great Ormond Street Hospital
